zh.lifeSummary: Adéle DE SOLRE於1059年出生。她和Ghislain II de Barbancon至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Adéle DE SOLRE于1059年出生。她和Ghislain II de Barbancon至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: SOLRE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Rafael, Carlos, Ana, Eduardo, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Miguel, Emilio, Fernando.
habitational name from any of the places called (El) Soler in Catalonia (Spain), from soler ‘ground, floor’ (from Late Latin solarium, a derivative of solum ‘bottom’, ‘ground’).

variants.name: Sole
meanings: English: from Middle English sol(e) ‘muddy pond, wallowing place’ (Old English sol). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived by a muddy pond, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Sole Farm in Great Bookham (Surrey).
English: nickname for someone who was unmarried, lived alone, or was lonely, from Middle English sol(e) ‘sole, single, lonely’ (Old French sol, Latin solus ‘alone’).

nameMeanings.name: Adéle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From French Adèle , an ancient name popular in medieval Europe because of the fame of a 7th-century saint, a daughter of the Frankish king Dagobert II. It is of Germanic origin, from adal ‘noble’ (a short form of a two-element name such as Adelheid; see Adelaide ). It was the name of William the Conqueror's youngest daughter ( c.1062–1137 ), who became the wife of Stephen of Blois. However, it died out in England in the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, being the name of a character in Johann Strauss's opera Die Fledermaus. Its popularity was further reinforced in the 1930s as the name of a character in the novels of Dornford Yates .

en.lifeSummary: Adéle DE SOLRE was born in 1059. She had at least 1 son with Ghislain II de Barbancon.

fr.lifeSummary: Adéle DE SOLRE est née en 1059. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Ghislain II de Barbancon.
